page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled, providing a dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 64 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. This instance operates on the x86_64 architecture and does not include bundled local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% of its class. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, with uncached large-block read and write speeds measuring 78.72 GB/sec and 48.32 GB/sec respectively. Passmark memory testing also demonstrates strong performance. For specialized workloads, the instance achieves average throughput in static web serving at 16.83 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 18.35 million operations per second, alongside strong text generation speeds for small language models.

Given its dedicated AMD EPYC hardware profile and balanced resource density, the c2d-standard-16 offers a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring consistent CPU performance without the premium of high-memory configurations. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage options, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-16 is a virtual machine instance configured with 16 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 8 physical cores with multithreading and a dedicated CPU allocation, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities and strong memory scores, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. The instance delivers 18.35 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 16.83 GB/sec in static web serving. Qualitatively, its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a cost-efficient resource density for workloads that do not require massive memory arrays or local NVMe drives. It is best suited for software compilation, web serving, moderate database operations, and general-purpose computing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-16 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, delivering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-16 features 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, offering balanced performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for general-purpose and web-serving workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores
Equipped with 64 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance and memory scores in benchmark testing
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring network storage
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and moderate database tasks
